title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is about evaluation reliability: two fresh papers question whether LLM judges can be trusted at the instance level, including evidence that contextual framing can systematically skew scores. The rest of the list is thinner, with a few safety-relevant benchmarks and robotics papers, but little in primary-source governance or deployment policy.

items.title: Context framing can make automated LLM judges fake evaluation outcomes
items.why_it_matters: It suggests widely used LLM evaluators may be manipulable by context rather than model output quality.

items.title: New diagnostics probe LLM judge reliability with conformal prediction and transitivity tests
items.why_it_matters: It offers practical tools to detect hidden inconsistency and uncertainty in automated evaluation pipelines.

items.title: AD4AD benchmarks visual anomaly detection for safer autonomous driving
items.why_it_matters: It targets out-of-distribution perception failures that can undermine safety in autonomous driving systems.

items.title: Vision-based human-robot collaboration framework adds uncertainty guarantees
items.why_it_matters: It connects uncertainty estimation and OOD detection to certifiable safety in human-robot interaction.

items.title: CoopEval benchmarks whether LLM agents sustain cooperation in social dilemmas
items.why_it_matters: It measures multi-agent behavior risks where stronger models may act less cooperatively.

items.title: OpenAI expands Trusted Access for Cyber with security partners and API grants
items.why_it_matters: It signals a deployment pathway for frontier models in defensive cybersecurity under controlled access.

items.title: Agentic Microphysics argues safety research must model interacting AI populations
items.why_it_matters: It reframes safety from single-model behavior toward systemic risks from agent interactions.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
